episode_text:
day=13; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Social with person on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=open mouth; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is cold-served fruit or dessert AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or attention-directed THEN no microwave BECAUSE repeated cold-food meals were served without reheating, consistent with intended cold serving. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV or working on a laptop/tablet, including front-, left-, or right-attention contexts, THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated warm savory meals in attention-directed contexts were reheated for 3 min, indicating a stable preference for thoroughly warming hot foods in those settings. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person in front or right THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed social savory meals in those contexts used moderate reheating, suggesting a social-setting preference for somewhat warm but not maximally hot food.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is general meals or warm savory meals in front-attention contexts THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated TV-in-front, laptop-in-front, and related front-attention meals selected front-occlusion minimization, indicating a stable preference to preserve the forward view when that view is important. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is cold fruit THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed front-social fruit meal did not prioritize occlusion avoidance, showing that front-social context alone does not always trigger occlusion minimization. IF dining context includes a focal activity or social partner to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE both right-attention and right-social meals selected right-occlusion minimization, showing a preference to preserve the attended right-side view. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE left-side attention meals prioritized preserving the attended left-side view.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal, social, or involves a concurrent visually attended activity AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow speed across nearly all observed meals, including social-right savory dining, indicating a generally comfort-first interaction style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is cold fruit THEN medium BECAUSE one observed left-laptop fruit meal used medium speed, suggesting that in at least some multitasking, low-risk meals the user may accept slightly faster motion.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is general, social, laptop-related, or includes fruit/dessert pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this option was selected across most observed meals, favoring reliable bite acquisition. IF food is a warm savory item in strips, pieces, sandwich bites, or nugget-like pieces AND affective state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV to the right, watching TV in front, social interaction with a person in front or right, or working on a laptop on the left THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated meals with solid savory bite-sized foods in these contexts used parallel skewering, suggesting a preference for narrower or more manageable bites for these foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person in front or right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE observed social meals used explicit confirmation, indicating a preference to verify bite acquisition when errors may be disruptive or visible. IF dining context involves watching TV to the right or in front, or working on a laptop positioned to the left or in front of the user, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE repeated meals in these contexts used explicit confirmation, indicating a broad preference for verification rather than skipping this step. IF dining context involves working on a laptop to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN no BECAUSE observed right-laptop meals skipped confirmation, suggesting a context-specific exception favoring reduced interaction burden there.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and all observed meals used inside-mouth transfer.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth distance does not apply, and the user has consistently selected inside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves working on a laptop, watching TV to the left, watching TV to the right, or social interaction including a person in front or right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE repeated meals in these contexts used quiet visual initiation cues, indicating a general preference for nonverbal signaling. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or watching TV in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is warm savory or general meals THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed meals in these front-attention contexts used multimodal initiation cues, suggesting that when the user is focused forward on a screen or task they sometimes prefer a stronger ready signal.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person in front or right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth readiness in social meals, showing that low-effort initiation outweighed possible ambiguity from conversation in the observed data. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV, including front-, left-, or right-side attention contexts, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected autocontinue in attention-divided contexts, indicating a stable preference to minimize interaction burden when occupied.

IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person in front or right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth readiness for drink initiation in social contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV, including front-, left-, or right-side attention contexts, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected autocontinue in attention-divided contexts, preferring lower-effort automation when occupied.

IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person in front or right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth readiness for wiping initiation in social contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV, including front-, left-, or right-side attention contexts, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected autocontinue in attention-divided contexts, consistent with preferring reduced interaction demand when occupied.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves working on a laptop, watching TV to the left, watching TV to the right, or social interaction including a person in front or right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE the user often selected nonverbal completion cues across these observed contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or watching TV in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is warm savory or general meals THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed meals in these front-attention contexts used multimodal completion cues, suggesting that when the user is focused on a screen or task they sometimes prefer a stronger completion signal.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected sensor-based bite completion detection in all observed meals, and this avoids button presses the user cannot reliably perform.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected retracting between bites in front-attention contexts including social-front, laptop-in-front, and TV-in-front meals, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to reduce prolonged visual obstruction when the attended view matters. IF dining context includes a focal activity or social partner to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE both right-attention and right-social meals used retracting, showing the same preference when preserving the right-side view matters. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE left-side attention meals also used retracting, extending this preference to left-side view preservation.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social, including social meals with a person in front, AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed social meals with dip used less dipping, consistent with limiting messiness in social settings. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed laptop meals with sauce or whipped cream used no dipping, indicating a stronger mess-avoidance preference when multitasking around equipment.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person in front or right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in observed social meals, balancing time to respond with meal efficiency. IF dining context involves watching TV to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE observed left-oriented attention meals used the moderate wait. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop to the left, working on a laptop to the right, or watching TV to the right or in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated attention-divided meals in those contexts used the longest wait, indicating a strong preference for extra time before automatic progression when attention is shared with another activity.
